channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Enbridge Sunrise Pipeline Expansion Begins Near Prince George, Boosting Regional Economy
item.description: The Enbridge Sunrise pipeline expansion project has officially started near Prince George, British Columbia, marking a significant step in Canada's energy infrastructure development. Federal Natural R...

item.title: Museveni's Grandson Graduates from Prestigious Exeter University
item.description: Mugina Karugire, the grandson of Ugandan President Yoweri Museveni, has graduated from the University of Exeter in the UK, a top-tier Russell Group institution. His mother, Natasha Museveni, shared th...

item.title: Tanzanian golfers excel at Arusha Open, with Nathwani and Olomi leading the way
item.description: Local golfer Jay Nathwani claimed victory at the Arusha Open Golf Tournament, securing the title with consistent performance across three rounds. The tournament, held at his home course, saw Nathwani ...

item.title: Farmers protest against proposed India-US trade deal with march to Delhi
item.description: Thousands of farmers from Haryana and Punjab have gathered at the Shambhu border, which separates the two states, ahead of a planned march to Delhi. The protesters are demanding action against the pro...

item.title: Pakistani Physician Elected Chair of US Religious Freedom Commission
item.description: Dr. Asif Mahmood, a Pakistani-born physician and human rights advocate, has been unanimously elected as the chair of the United States Commission on International Religious Freedom (USCIRF) for the 20...

item.title: Wilderdark: A First-Person Survival Horror Game Set in a Mysterious Island
item.description: Junkfish, the Malaysian indie game studio, announced Wilderdark, its first-person survival horror adventure, set for release on Steam on 3 November 2026. Players are deployed by a mysterious organizat...

item.title: Johannesburg High Court to Deliver Judgment in Usindiso Building Arson Case
item.description: The Johannesburg High Court is set to deliver judgment in the case of alleged Usindiso building arsonist Sithembiso Mdlalose, who faces 76 counts of murder and 12 attempted murder charges linked to th...

item.title: Cassava Technologies Boosts Africa's Cloud Resilience with Microsoft Azure in Johannesburg
item.description: Cassava Technologies has strengthened Africa’s cloud infrastructure by designating Africa Data Centres (ADC) in Johannesburg as a Microsoft Azure ExpressRoute Metro peering location. This makes ADC th...

item.title: Regulatory gaps allow some Australian wellness clinics to supply unapproved peptides and anti-ageing injections
item.description: The article examines how some Australian online wellness and longevity clinics are supplying unapproved peptides and anti-ageing injections by relying on legal exemptions intended for limited clinical...

item.title: Andy Burnham's Cabinet Reshuffle and Chancellor John Healey's Spending Challenges
item.description: The article details the reshuffling of the UK government following Keir Starmer's resignation, with Andy Burnham appointed as the new Prime Minister. Key changes include the appointment of John Healey...

item.title: Marcos Appoints Antonio Nafarrete as AFP Chief Staff Amid Military Transition
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. has appointed Lieutenant General Antonio Nafarrete as the new chief of staff of the Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP), replacing General Romeo Brawner Jr., who is se...

item.title: Laptop Solves Quantum-Only Problem Using Tensor Networks
item.description: Researchers have demonstrated that a standard laptop can solve complex problems previously believed to require quantum computers, using advanced tensor network algorithms. The study, published in *Sci...

item.title: Sunny Hostin's son stopped for trespassing on railroad property
item.description: A body camera video released by Fox News Digital captures 'The View' co-host Sunny Hostin interacting with Metropolitan Transportation Authority (MTA) police after her son, Gabriel Hostin, was stopped...

item.title: Romanda Hombir's Coronation Sparks Identity Debate in South Africa
item.description: Romanda Hombir, a 27-year-old clinical audiologist from Mpumalanga, was crowned Miss World South Africa 2026 at the Barnyard Theatre in Pretoria. Her victory has sparked online debates about her eligi...

item.title: Zimbabwe's Businessman Pedzai 'Scott' Sakupwanya: Key Player in Mining and Politics
item.description: Pedzai 'Scott' Sakupwanya, a prominent Zimbabwean businessman with interests in mining, energy, and logistics, has become a key figure close to President Emmerson Mnangagwa. As a ZANU-PF financier tur...

item.title: Rigathi Gachagua's fashion evolution: Embracing casual style in public appearances
item.description: Former Deputy President Rigathi Gachagua has sparked conversations about his evolving fashion choices, particularly after recently wearing a high-end Rodd & Gunn shirt at a public event. The green and...

item.title: EFCC Says Immigration CG Kemi Nandap Is Not Under Investigation, Confirms Cases Against Some Immigration Officers
item.description: The Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC) has dismissed reports claiming that the Comptroller-General of the Nigeria Immigration Service (NIS), Kemi Nandap, is being investigated over allege...

item.title: WordPress Vulnerabilities Remain a Major Security Risk Despite Recent Patches
item.description: Hackers are actively exploiting critical security vulnerabilities in WordPress that were patched last week, putting millions of websites at risk. Despite WordPress' forced updates for versions 6.9.0 t...

item.title: BC Ferries reunites lost donkey with owner after community effort
item.description: A stuffed pink donkey named 'Churro the Burro' became an unexpected star after being left behind on a BC Ferry. The incident, which occurred during a sailing from Swartz Bay to the Southern Gulf Islan...

item.title: KPMG appoints John Sams as chief executive after leadership changes linked to audit leaks scandal
item.description: KPMG has appointed long-serving partner John Sams as its new chief executive, formally filling the leadership position left vacant after Andrew Yates resigned in the wake of the firm's audit leaks sca...
